title: colutea persica
intro: Colutea persica, also known as the Bladder Campion or Bladder Pea, is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family. It is a shrub-like perennial herb native to Asia and Europe, valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Colutea persica can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in traditional medicine to promote wound healing and alleviate symptoms of menopause.
